# Corpus v0 — Haitian Creole (Workstream A)

*Build snapshot 2026-07-20 · run `full`. Generated by `ml/corpus/` — see [../../docs/phase-0.md](../../docs/phase-0.md) (Workstreams 0 + A) and [../../docs/plan.md](../../docs/plan.md) §5.2. Rights & splits fixed first in [`../corpus/rights.yaml`](../corpus/rights.yaml) / [`../corpus/splits.yaml`](../corpus/splits.yaml).*

## What is actually there

Corpus v0 (rights-clear sources only, deduplicated) is **143,940 documents** — **497,397,899 bytes**, **484,340,940 chars**, **91,129,087 whitespace words**, **151,638,173 tokens** under the reference tokenizer `o200k_base` (o200k_base, the same unit Workstream C reports).

The phase-0 plan's ~90–100M-token figure is an **estimate, not a target**: this is what the clean build actually contains. (MADLAD's own count is ~84.3M whitespace-ish "tokens" over 110,443 raw docs — a *different* unit from o200k tokens; the two are not comparable.)

## Sizes

Four units per docs/phase-0.md A6. "Tokens" throughout = `o200k_base` (named reference tokenizer), **not** our future Kreyòl BPE (Workstream B).

### By source

| source | docs | bytes | chars | whitespace words | o200k tokens |
|---|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|
| madlad_400_ht_clean | 108,246 | 458,301,334 | 446,213,588 | 84,476,265 | 139,332,019 |
| ht_wikipedia | 35,659 | 39,095,374 | 38,126,196 | 6,652,569 | 12,305,747 |
| owned_proverbs | 35 | 1,191 | 1,156 | 253 | 407 |
| **total** | 143,940 | 497,397,899 | 484,340,940 | 91,129,087 | 151,638,173 |

| origin | docs | bytes | chars | whitespace words | o200k tokens |
|---|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|
| web_crawl | 108,246 | 458,301,334 | 446,213,588 | 84,476,265 | 139,332,019 |

| genre | docs | bytes | chars | whitespace words | o200k tokens |
|---|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|
| web | 108,246 | 458,301,334 | 446,213,588 | 84,476,265 | 139,332,019 |
| encyclopedic | 35,659 | 39,095,374 | 38,126,196 | 6,652,569 | 12,305,747 |

> `origin=web_crawl` and `genre=web` are additions to docs/plan.md §5.2's written enums, used for MADLAD crawl text so we don't assert authorship (`authored_kreyol`) or a translation origin we can't verify. See `corpus/schema.py`.

## Deduplication

Exact (content hash) → near-dup (MinHash/LSH, 5-gram word shingles, Jaccard ≥0.8, 128 perms) at document level, then paragraph-level exact-dup across sources. Survivor priority **owned > wikipedia > crawl**, tie-broken by longer text then doc id.

| stage | removed |
|---|--:|
| input documents | 180,166 |
| document exact-dup removed | 10,552 |
| document near-dup removed | 25,670 |
| whole docs dropped (all paragraphs duplicated) | 4 |
| **final documents** | **143,940** |
| document removal rate | 20.1% |
| paragraph segments removed (boilerplate, across docs) | 12,360 |
| docs edited by paragraph dedup | 6,559 |
| duplicate-map clusters recorded | 2,262 |

Survivors by source priority class: `owned`=35, `wikipedia`=35,659, `crawl`=108,246.

The **duplicate map** (`data/interim/full/dedup/duplicate_map.jsonl`, git-ignored) records every cluster: id, level, survivor, why the survivor won, and all members — so nothing is silently discarded. Paragraph-level **near**-dup is intentionally not run at full scale (a MinHash per paragraph is ~1–2M objects); it is covered indirectly by document near-dup + paragraph exact-dup.

## Filtering (source-specific)

Thresholds differ by source (a single floor would delete proverbs and conversational text while under-filtering crawl). Wikipedia bot-stubs are **flagged, not dropped**.

| source | in | kept | dropped | drop reasons |
|---|--:|--:|--:|---|
| madlad | 110,443 | 110,391 | 52 | mojibake=34, too_short_chars=7, high_symbol_ratio=3, high_digit_ratio=8 |
| htwiki | 73,457 | 69,740 | 3,717 | too_short_chars=3704, too_few_words=8, high_symbol_ratio=1, empty_after_boilerplate=3, high_digit_ratio=1 |
| proverbs | 35 | 35 | 0 | — |

## Wikipedia bot-stubs

Haitian Wikipedia is heavily bot-generated. Flagged (short + template-driven, or a geo/species-stub opening) in the FINAL corpus: **20,427 / 35,659 = 57.3%** of surviving Wikipedia docs. They are kept (real Kreyòl, low information density) but tagged `wiki_bot_stub=true` so downstream stages can down-weight or exclude them. This is a heuristic flag, not ground truth.

## Owned proverbs (teachable) + probe holdout

CreolePro's 50 proverbs are split deterministically (seed 20260720): **35 teachable** proverbs are in this corpus (`split=train`, `genre=proverb`, `origin=authored_kreyol`); **15 probe** proverbs are held out in `data/eval/proverbs_probe.jsonl` and never enter training or the tokenizer — they are Station 2's "who knows the real proverb?" set. Only the Kreyòl text enters the corpus (English gloss + category are metadata). Tiny by volume, but the highest-quality, authored Kreyòl in the build.

**Probe contamination check.** The holdout removes only the *owned* copy of each probe proverb; a famous proverb can still appear inside a MADLAD/Wikipedia doc. Exact-substring scan of the training corpus: **4 / 15 probe proverbs also occur in training text.** This is expected for well-known proverbs and is why splits.yaml keeps an n-gram contamination check as the training-time backstop; for Station 2, prefer probe proverbs with 0 training hits (or accept that the model may have seen the phrasing via crawl).

| probe # | train hits | proverb |
|--:|--:|---|
| 10 | 5 | Sak vid pa kanpe. |
| 19 | 2 | Bay kou bliye, pote mak sonje. |
| 1 | 1 | Dèyè mòn gen mòn. |
| 36 | 1 | Pitit se richès malere. |
| 11 | 0 | Zanmi nan nesesite se zanmi tout bon vre. |
| 12 | 0 | Bouch granmoun santi, men pawòl li pa santi. |
| 27 | 0 | Twòp prese pa fè jou louvri. |
| 28 | 0 | Chen gen fòs nan je mèt li. |
| 29 | 0 | Malfini kouri di li wè, poul kouri di li pa vle. |
| 30 | 0 | Kabrit ki gen twòp mèt mouri nan solèy. |
| 31 | 0 | Lè chat pa la, rat pran kay. |
| 38 | 0 | Lajan nan men, zanmi toupatou. |
| 42 | 0 | Moun ki renmen ou di ou verite. |
| 45 | 0 | Dlo ki koule pa janm tounen nan sous. |
| 47 | 0 | Wè pa di konnen. |

## MADLAD source domains

**Not available.** The released MADLAD-400 ht `clean` JSONL carries only a `text` field — no per-document URL or domain — so a top-domains breakdown of the crawl slice is not possible from the data. (The crawl origin is visible in the text of many docs — supplier listings, product pages — but that is not a reliable domain signal.)

## Quality audit — machine first pass

Deterministic stratified sample of **200 docs** (seed 20260720) across sources × length bands. **Rates below are MACHINE-ESTIMATED (fasttext lid.176 + heuristics) and pending human review.**

| scope | n | wrong_lang | wrong_lang≥300 | low_conf_ht | boilerplate | unreadable |
|---|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|
| **overall** | 200 | 25.5% | 20.7% | 71.0% | 0.0% | 0.0% |
| madlad_400_ht_clean | 151 | 12.6% | 12.0% | 84.8% | 0.0% | 0.0% |
| ht_wikipedia | 48 | 64.6% | 75.0% | 29.2% | 0.0% | 0.0% |
| owned_proverbs | 1 | 100.0% | — | 0.0% | 0.0% | 0.0% |

**Reading these honestly:** `boilerplate`/`unreadable` are ~0 — a good sign the filter did its job. The `wrong_language` numbers are an **upper bound that mostly measures lid.176's weakness on Kreyòl**, not contamination: eyeballing the flagged docs, they are overwhelmingly genuine Kreyòl encyclopedic stubs dense with French/Spanish proper nouns (foreign actor names, film titles) that tip langid to `fr`/`es`/`tl`. Restricting to docs ≥300 chars does not fix it — langid simply under-detects Haitian Creole. `low_conf_ht` (langid picked `ht` but with low confidence) is high for the same reason. **Human review is the actual audit; this is the machine first pass that tells a reviewer where to look.** The full sample with text is in the git-ignored `ml/reports/audit_sample.jsonl`; a committable review sheet is [`audit_sample_review.md`](audit_sample_review.md).

## Caveats & what needs human review

- **MADLAD license unresolved (CC-BY-4.0 vs ODC-BY).** Redistribution stays blocked and MADLAD text is not excerpted in committed files (audit snippets redacted) until resolved — see `rights.yaml`. Analysis/tokenizer/model-training are permitted under either candidate license.
- **MADLAD is crawl, not authored.** Visible commercial boilerplate (supplier/product pages) and occasional mojibake survive filtering; this is translation-shaped, machine-aligned web text, not authored Kreyòl. `origin=web_crawl`.
- **Wikipedia is bot-stub-heavy** (see above) — flagged, not authored-labeled.
- **langid is unreliable for Kreyòl** — the wrong-language rate is a tooling artifact as much as a corpus signal. Needs a fluent reviewer.
- **Owned proverbs** (CreolePro's 50) are the only authored, non-crawl, non-encyclopedic source: 35 teachable proverbs entered training; 15 probe proverbs are held out (`data/eval/proverbs_probe.jsonl`, never trained) for Station 2. They are a tiny slice by volume but the highest-quality Kreyòl in the corpus.
- Quarantined sources (MIT-Haiti, dictionary) and eval-only FLORES+ are **not** in this corpus, by design.

## Reproduce

```bash
cd ml && uv sync
uv run python -m corpus.run --sample   # 1% smoke test, end to end
uv run python -m corpus.run            # full build + audit + this report
```

Sources pinned: MADLAD `allenai/MADLAD-400` @ `9d886a76bd8f` (ht clean); htwiki dump `20260701`. Seeds: sample 20260720, audit 20260720.
